reviews.review_msg: Mike's class was the last class I took before switching my major to economics. The focus on current events was helpful for working through what was happening at the time. The project was great, but picking good groupmembers was tough.
Greatest pro ever Didn't review the midterm because of world cup, still  got B, and final was very easy, assignments were hard, but he was so helpful would love to take with him again
hands down for the chillest econ professor in the school
final exam is extremely easy
He is great a professor. Willing to help students. The exams overall are fair. If do all questions provided by him, you are great to go!
Great great prof!!!!!! take his class!!!!
best prof in sfu. He is very nice and very helpful for the assignments.
Very helpful for assignments and exams. True that the midterm was way too long, but he did gave us a short final. Honestly, econ courses are all hard, you can't find any easy grade courses
Coolest econ prof met in sfu, office hours are chill and helpful. The assignments and exams are more than fair.
midterm was amazingly hard and way too long. No one left before exam ended.

To be honest, nice teacher in class. His class is interesting, and he will answer your question very fast. But! textbook is useless and tons of very academic readings. Exam is hard because sometimes you have no idea where the questions come from. After his class, you would have no idea on how to study and review for exam

OK, He is nice guy as other said.he explain something and the exam come from somewhere (NOT from the book or his lecture) in other words, his lecture and the book are useless. in the exam less than 40 from lectures and book other 60% from somewhere else. he is very hard marker. the class is easy but the problem with him
Best professor I've had at SFU. He's funny, knowledgeable and despite what others are claiming, he has very reasonable exams.

one of the worst class I took ever, the class is easy but his exam way hard. he explain something in the class but the exam question came from somewhere I don't know from where.. if you want a good grade don't take his class.also, he is a hard marker

He's a very knowledgeable prof but teaching wise, he can definitely get very confusing. Difficult to understand in his class since he talks pretty fast. Extra reading and studying is definitely needed. The textbook and his slides are the best study material. Going to class isn't that helpful, stay updated with materialand do the practice problems.
Best prof ever! So easy, very clear and concise. MUST HAVE FOR ECON 103! (and 102)

I studied my behind off and got a B+ no thanks to this prof. I went to every lecture and it's really useless 8:30 in the morning. He sends out practice questions like 2 days BEFORE THE EXAM, that's really helpful.. If you have him just pay attention to those questions he sends it's on the exams

Nice prof and fair exams. He is very knowledged. He knows a lot and I think he is really want to teach us all he knows, that is why he talk fast and sometimes run out of topic.  Anyway, he is a good prof.
His exams are long and hard to finish...Some questions are based on what he talked about in class and not in the book or notes... and he doesn't double check his equations before exam... horrible

Very knowledgeable, not just about the course material but everything in general, making the lecture far more interesting. Rote studying for exams will NOT work, as questions refer to scenarios not explicitly described in lecture. As long as you understand the reasoning covered in class though, you'll do well. Wish he was teaching this summer...

He is irresponsible. He always uploads the notes late and does not use projector or ppt. However, his handwriting is horrible! He likes to run out of topic, and it's really hard to follow. Sometimes he does not even know the stuffs himself.
Boring lecture, irresponsible TAs, bad marking scheme, disorganized instructor and unfair exams.......what can you expect? better study by yourself buddy..

He has to work hard on his presenting skill! he talks way too fast.It's super hard to follow him. Plus, he's not well prepared for the class. He uploads his lecture notes a few days after his lecture.you cannot study ahead and you have no idea what he's going to teach because he's not following his syllabus.exams=unfair&impossible to finish

His lecture is quite useless and he talks really fast. He shows you bunch of graphs that you won't be tested in lecture and you never know which chapter he 's teaching. Everything is so not organized and his syllabus is useless. You will find something new in your exams, Duh!! Exams was unfair...lots of typo...not recommended

I don't know about you guys but I didn't finish the midterm today.... In a 2 hour exam time, 1 hour into the exam "We have a mistake in #1" WHOLE class sighs and groans 20mins left in exam "We have a mistake in #3" ..... REALLY?

You should be fine if you are prepared to self-study. That way you probably can save two hours of your week too unless you are particularly drawn to a self-absorbed guy mumbling information to himself in an elusive air.  However if you're in for some solid grounding for your Economics career and need a boost from somebody, avoid at all cost.
Never replied to emails and is certainly poor with time management. Does not seem to care about the class at all. I would never recommend this prof.
He never respond any emails and seems not care about the class at all.

He is a very talent pro. He really loves talking and he talks fast... When you ask him a question he just keeps talkingggg and then you find out you cannot follow him any more. His brain runs fast.But he is very nice and friendly. :D Always willing to help.
took 102 was way too hard
